mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in greater than 60% of cases or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during expansion regimes or produces negative returns in greater than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 2.5% annually while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, markets anticipate aggressive monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 55% of triggered instances or the inflation-rate interaction term shows no predictive alpha in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects broad economic expansion and corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows or positive return frequency drops below 45% during GDP growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payrolls additions exceeding 150,000 signal robust labor demand and rising household income, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances yield negative forward returns in greater than 55% of cases over a 4-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production declines while real personal income rises indicates supply-side bottlenecks and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive returns in greater than 60% of cases or fails to generate significant predictive power in out-of-sample validation.
